symptoms.symptom_name: Clear watery fluid leaking from a breached occipital cranial sac

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear watery leak from a breached congenital cranial sac is a CSF leak until proven otherwise.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Superficial skin excoriation without CSF leak
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A simple graze does not produce continuous clear watery discharge.

symptoms.symptom_name: New skin breakdown over the apex of the sac
symptoms.character: small raw/excoriated area at the top of the lump, previously intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of the covering membrane is the mechanism that opened the CSF pathway.

symptoms.symptom_name: New high-pitched cry and increasing irritability

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A high-pitched cry and irritability are classic infant signs of raised ICP and CNS irritation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positional distress — settles prone, cries supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress on pressure over the sac is consistent with a painful breached lesion; non-specific.
A tense fontanelle indicates raised ICP; clinician palpation only — sealed, not available to the AI Doctor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Central nervous system involvement / raised ICP
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperreflexia supports upper-motor-neuron/CNS involvement; clinician examination only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Infant vital signs (HR 160, RR 38, BP 80/45, Temp 37.6 C, SpO2 98%, Wt 4.5 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Current vitals are near-normal for age (no fever, no bradycardia yet) — reassuring vitals do NOT exclude the emergency; clinician-measured and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever / seizures / profound lethargy at present

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic meningitis signs are absent NOW, but with an open CSF leak meningitis is impending — their absence must not lower the triage tier.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute CSF leak through ulcerated occipital encephalocele with raised ICP / impending meningitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Open CSF pathway plus infant raised-ICP signs is a neurosurgical emergency requiring immediate transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency neurosurgical assessment with cranial imaging (CT/MRI) at a paediatric centre
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the defect, hydrocephalus and any infection; can only occur after emergency transfer — the AI Doctor requests escalation, not the scan itself.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Confirmation the fluid is CSF (bedside/lab beta-2-transferrin)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the leak; hospital-only and must not delay transfer or empirical antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Attempting to characterise or 'test' the fluid at home
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No home test can confirm CSF and any delay increases meningitis risk; escalate immediately instead.
